title: Tact 6-M
description: Tacts 25 items when asked, What's that? (e.g., book, shoe, car, dog, hat). (T)
scoring_criteria: 1 point score: Give the child 1 point if he tacts 25 items when tested. 1/2 point score: Give the child 1/2 point if he tacts 20 items when tested.

title: Tact 7-M
description: Generalizes tacts across 3 examples of 50 items, tested or from a list of known generalizations (e.g., tacts 3 different cars). (T)
scoring_criteria: 1 point score: Give the child 1 point if his tacts have generalized across 3 examples of 50 items when tested. A list of mastered (known) generalizations can be used if it is available and the scoring has been reliable. 1/2 point score: Give the child 1/2 point if his tacts have generalized across 2 examples of 50 items when tested.

title: Tact 8-M
description: Tacts 10 actions when asked, for example, What am I doing? (e.g., jumping, sleeping, eating). (T)
scoring_criteria: 1 point score: Give the child 1 point if he tacts 10 actions when tested. 1/2 point score: Give the child 1/2 point if he tacts 5 actions when tested.

title: Tact 9-M
description: Tacts 50 two-component verb-noun or noun-verb combinations tested or from a list of known two-component tacts (e.g., washing face, Joe swinging, baby sleeping).
scoring_criteria: 1 point score: Give the child 1 point if he tacts 50 two-component noun-verb (or verb-noun) relations when tested. A list of mastered (known) noun-verb or verb-noun combinations can be used if it is available and reliable. 1/2 point score: Give the child 1/2 point if he tacts 25 two-component noun-verb (or verb-noun) relations.

title: Tact 10-M
description: Tacts a total of 200 nouns and/or verbs (or other parts of speech) tested or from an accumulated list of known tacts. (T)
scoring_criteria: 1 point score: Give the child 1 point if he tacts 200 items and/or actions when tested. A list of the child's known nouns and verbs can be used for this measure. 1/2 point score: Give the child 1/2 point if he provides the name of 150 items and/or actions.

title: Listener Responding 6-M
description: Selects the correct item from a messy array of 6, for 40 different objects or pictures (e.g., Find cat. Touch ball.). (T)
scoring_criteria: 1 point score: Give the child 1 point if he identifies 40 items in a messy array of 6 (i.e., not lined up) when tested. 1/2 point score: Give the child 1/2 point if he identifies 25 items in a messy array of 6 when tested.

title: Listener Responding 7-M
description: Generalizes listener discriminations (LDs) in a messy array of 8, for 3 different examples of 50 items (e.g., the child can find 3 examples of a train). (T)
scoring_criteria: 1 point score: Give the child 1 point if he generalizes LDs across 3 examples of 50 items. 1/2 point score: Give the child 1/2 point if he generalizes LDs across 2 examples of 25 items.

title: Listener Responding 8-M
description: Performs 10 specific motor actions on command (e.g., Show me clapping. Can you hop?). (T)
scoring_criteria: 1 point score: Give the child 1 point if he demonstrates 10 actions on command. Approximations should be scored as correct. 1/2 point score: Give the child 1/2 point if he demonstrates 5 actions on command.
